parsed_review.summary: CP00-358 is a descriptor-only RP11 time-expense failure-tail-slice pack (Risk B, 40 units, RP11.P07.M04.S17-RP11.P07.M06.S14). All metadata reconciles across the review baseline, live closeout-pack-plan.json, time-expense-core-contract.json (current_pack, failure_tail_slice_descriptor projection, and historical_pack_bindings tail), registry binding, manifest, and CP358 requirements. Deliverable distribution (test 4 / security_audit 4 / hermes_evidence 3 / failure_recovery 24 / implementation 3 / fixture 1 / claude_review 1 = 40) and micro-title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice 4 / Permission And Audit Binding 22 / Synthetic Fixture Set 14 = 40) verified against the included unit list. All 7 required public exports exist in service.js/validators.js and re-export via index.js wildcards; the test imports them from the package index, so node --test passing confirms index_export_check end-to-end. Coverage and descriptor validators are sound and backed by the inherited ROW_EXTRAS chain, which defines every leak-guard field the validator checks (executes_unit_test_runtime_paths, dispatches_integration_smoke_runtime, audit_hint_detail_included, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, cross_tenant_access_allowed, permission_decision_detail_included, acquires_runtime_lock, performs_retry_runtime, performs_rollback_runtime, blocked_claim_detail_included, claude_final_approval_claimed, human_approval_route_required_before_runtime). No-real-data/no-write boundaries hold: the source builds only frozen descriptor objects with no fs/network/db/exec operations; the no-write attestation chain (promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false) is unbroken CP342->CP358. Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, human approval required before runtime opening, no enterprise-trust claim from local validation. Hermes patterns are limited to deterministic review-packet/receipt evidence strings (gate H11) with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false everywhere, not product runtime. production_ready is correctly false (descriptor verified, pending final closeout); the Hermes production_ready_candidate is a coverage/slice validity signal, not an enterprise-trust claim. CP178+ review-hardening blockers (failed auth, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields) are encoded in forbidden_review_evidence and the auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON). No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer; this PASS is a review recommendation only and human/final approval remains required for the enterprise trust claim.
